steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main topic of the passage. The passage describes how both Cisneros and Castillo embed Spanish words in their English-language fiction without translating them, and how context makes those words accessible to all English readers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The difficulty of translating Sandra Cisneros's works into languages other than English.: The passage is about Spanish words inside English texts, not about translating those texts into other languages. ✗
  • Choice B — The commercial success of English-language literature around the world.: Commercial success is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — The enduring popularity of Ana Castillo's works in Mexico.: Regional popularity is not discussed anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — The use of non-English vocabulary in two English-language literary works.: This matches perfectly — the passage is entirely about how Cisneros and Castillo both use Spanish words within English prose. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
